question.text: Losses from exchange-traded derivative transactions can be set off against:

answer.text: Any non-speculative business income during the year (except salary), and carried forward for 8 assessment years against non-speculative business income
Only speculative business income, with carry forward of 4 assessment years
Salary income and capital gains without restriction
All types of income including salary and house property income

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Since the Finance Act 2005 amended Section 43(5), exchange-traded derivative transactions are non-speculative. Therefore, losses can be set off against any non-speculative business income during the year (except salary income). Unabsorbed losses can be carried forward for 8 assessment years and set off against non-speculative business income only. The return of income must be filed on or before the due date to avail carry-forward.
**The Trap:** The "only against speculative income / 4 years" rule applies to genuinely speculative transactions — NOT to exchange-traded derivatives (which are non-speculative since 2005). This is a major exam trap. Also, STT paid is eligible as a deduction under the IT Act.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Set-off rules for derivative losses are commonly tested in Series VIII and I.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Exchange F&O losses: set off vs any business income (not salary), carry forward 8 years. File ITR on time to preserve carry-forward rights.
